title: pseudocytisus integrifolius subsp. ibericus
intro: Pseudocytisus integrifolius subsp. ibericus, also known as Iberian cytisus, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the pea family (Fabaceae). It is native to the Iberian Peninsula and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The plant is rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to have potential health benefits such as reducing oxidative stress and improving cardiovascular health. Pseudocytisus integrifolius subsp. ibericus can be prepared as a herbal tea or infusion, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including digestive issues and skin conditions.
